subject: Configure Nginx Reverse Proxy
description: Create nginx/nginx.conf with: worker_processes auto, events { worker_connections 1024 }, http { include mime.types, sendfile on, gzip on (gzip_types for js/css/json), server { listen 80, server_name _, location /health { access_log off; return 200 'OK'; add_header Content-Type text/plain; }, location / { root /usr/share/nginx/html, try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html } } }. IMPORTANT: The /health endpoint is REQUIRED for Docker health checks in docker-compose.prod.yml. Create nginx/security-headers.conf with: X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-XSS-Protection 1;mode=block, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Content-Security-Policy (allow Supabase domain, Google OAuth). Create nginx/ssl.conf template for HTTPS configuration. Disable server_tokens. Configure caching headers for static assets (1 year for hashed files, no-cache for index.html).
result_summary:
Configured the production nginx reverse proxy and committed it as `a159abe` (`[task-041] Configure nginx reverse proxy`). Updated `nginx/nginx.conf` to disable `server_tokens`, keep the required `/health` endpoint, apply SPA fallback, serve `index.html` and `metadata.json` with no-cache headers, and cache hashed Expo assets under `/_expo/static/` for one year with immutable cache-control. Added `nginx/security-headers.conf` with the requested security headers plus a CSP that allows the Supabase domains and Google OAuth endpoints, and added `nginx/ssl.conf` as a dormant HTTPS server template for the follow-on SSL task. Updated `Dockerfile` to copy the full `nginx/` directory into `/etc/nginx/` so the new include files exist inside the runtime image.

Verification: `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed; `npm run typecheck` passed. `nginx -t` could not be run in this environment because the `nginx` binary is unavailable. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the task files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: input validation, authentication, authorization, and sanitized client error handling were not applicable because this task only adds static web-server configuration, not app endpoints. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path test harness exists for nginx config in this repo yet; verification was limited to web export, typecheck, manual config inspection, and the commit.
